Off Calendar May-13-2015 Granted Per Written Order

Off Calendar May-06-2015 Continued To May-13-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-04-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Apperances: Roger Gross for Petitioner Treacy Malloy.

The Court stated that each heir is entitled to notice. Mr. Gross reported that used an heir search firm that conducted an informal search and was unable to locate any family members. The Court clarified and stated that the mother's side was found, but the father has a very common name and the search was abandoned.

Mr. Gross stated that there is no information on the father, that his name was misspelled on the birth certificate, that his middle name was left off, and a deeper search would require 3 to 8 months and more money. The Court granted the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary with full authority (subject to receipt and review of the order). Bond will be waiver per provision in the will. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-19-2014 Continued To Apr-02-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Gross for Treacy Malloy.

Mr. Gross explained that the house is in terrible disrepair, that there are liens against the house by Medi Cal, that the back stairs are bad and that the house needs to be cleaned out. Mr. Gross also stated that bills needs to be paid, and that the Executor nominee has been paying the bills but is unable to continue doing so. The Court granted issuance of special letters without full authority (subject to review of the order). Bond is set at $10,000.00. The special letters will expire upon further order of the Court. Attachment 3d2 will read "with special powers to preserve the property located at 310 Duncan Street, San Francisco, CA 94131 and to marshal the checking account in the amount of $8,000.00". Further efforts to locate family members was also discussed. The Petition for Probate of Will and Letters Testamentary was continued to April 2, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
